description: IP 91.200.81.47 appears in email_1 X-Originating-IP/Received headers and in network_logs outbound traffic at 01:58, 02:07, and 02:14 UTC - same bulk mailer infrastructure. This is a valid cross-evidence link.
forensicLesson: IP address correlation across evidence sources strengthens infrastructure attribution, but it must be tied to exact timestamps and should not be overstated as human identity proof.

description: audio_call.mp3 creation timestamp 02:14 AM matches the Tor exit node connection in network_logs at 02:14 AM - strong temporal co-occurrence.
forensicLesson: Timestamp alignment across unrelated evidence types (audio metadata + firewall logs) creates a strong temporal chain - but correlation alone does not prove causation.

description: invoice_fraud.pdf was created at 01:47:22 UTC and modified at 02:01:55 UTC. The network logs show outbound activity from the same unregistered workstation minutes later, including Tor/exfiltration traffic at 02:14:33 UTC. The timeline is suspicious but still insufficient to prove the same actor without payload-level evidence.
forensicLesson: Temporal proximity is weak evidence. Without a direct data artifact linking the invoice file to the exfiltrated payload, this connection is circumstantial.
